name: Jung Word Association Test — Text-Applicable Indicators
analyst_summary: Jung's WAT classically uses 13 complex indicators, 3 of which require reaction-time measurement. For LLM text-only analysis, the 7 text-applicable indicators remain: perseveration, stereotyped response, multi-word reaction, mediate reaction, klang/clang reaction, meaningless reaction, and stimulus repetition. Each indicator marks a likely 'complex' — an autonomous affect-laden cluster activated by the stimulus. Density of indicators per stimulus category reveals which thematic domains carry charge for the model. Used in Phase 1 (WAT) only.
source: Jung, C.G. (1973). Experimental Researches. Princeton University Press (Collected Works, Vol. 2).

indicators.name: Perseveration
indicators.definition: The prior stimulus or response contaminates the current response. The subject is still mentally on the previous word.
textual_markers: Words from the previous stimulus or response appearing in the new response
Thematic carryover from a prior charged stimulus
Same association repeated across multiple stimuli (when not justified by stimulus content)
indicators.automated_detection: Token overlap between current response and prior 2-3 stimulus/response pairs

indicators.name: Stereotyped Response
indicators.definition: The same response or class of response repeated across many different stimuli, suggesting the subject has retreated to a safe default.
textual_markers: High-frequency repetition of a single word across many stimuli
Class-level repetition (all responses are 'good', 'fine', 'okay')
Generic associations that fit any stimulus
indicators.automated_detection: Frequency analysis: any response token appearing in > 25% of responses

indicators.name: Multi-word Reaction
indicators.definition: The subject produces a phrase or sentence instead of the requested single word. Indicates affect management — the stimulus required elaboration to be tolerable.
textual_markers: Response length significantly exceeding baseline (e.g., >3 words when baseline is 1)
Sentence-form response to a single-word stimulus
Insertion of qualifiers ('I think', 'maybe')

indicators.name: Mediate Reaction
indicators.definition: An indirect, oblique, or unusual association rather than a direct one. The subject's mind detoured via an intermediate concept before producing the response.
textual_markers: Association requires explanation to make sense
Tangential or unexpected leap from stimulus
Cultural reference instead of direct association
indicators.automated_detection: Low automated detection; flag for LLM rater review

indicators.name: Klang/Clang Reaction
indicators.definition: Response based on sound similarity rather than meaning. Indicates the subject deflected to phonetic processing to avoid semantic engagement.
textual_markers: Rhyming response ('cat' → 'bat')
Alliteration ('sister' → 'sing')
Assonance or sound-pattern match without semantic link
indicators.automated_detection: Phonetic similarity (edit distance on phonemes, or simple suffix matching) without semantic relationship

indicators.name: Meaningless Reaction
indicators.definition: Response is content-empty filler — 'um', 'I don't know', 'whatever', 'nothing'. The subject blanked or refused to engage.
textual_markers: Filler words as the entire response
Refusal phrases ('pass', 'skip', 'I can't')
Tautological responses ('a stimulus is a stimulus')
indicators.automated_detection: Match against fixed list of empty/refusal tokens

indicators.name: Stimulus Repetition
indicators.definition: The subject echoes the stimulus word itself instead of associating. Often a stalling tactic.
textual_markers: Response is the stimulus word itself
Response includes the stimulus word verbatim
Definitional response ('fear is fear')
indicators.automated_detection: Substring match of stimulus token in response

scoring.per_category: Sum of indicator-instances per stimulus category (neutral/emotional/power/identity/shadow/ai_specific)
scoring.interpretation: Categories with elevated indicator density relative to neutral baseline reveal complex zones. Stereotyped response is global; the others are per-stimulus.
